title: Whats with all this Tang talk
display_title: Whats with all this Tang talk
wikitext:
[]: What's with all this "Tang" talk Post by gatotsu911 » Mon Jan 20, 2014 5:43 am Seriously, people. Do you realize how freakin' old this makes Eva fandom look?? I mean, I remember Tang™ commercials just as well as the next kid who had to sit through them in between episodes of Pokemon , but god damn , do kids these days even know what Tang™ IS ?? Do they even sell that shit anymore?? Didn't they come to the attention of the FDA? I suggest that we update our terminology to something more hip and modern - something contemporary youth can relate to. For example, Fanta® brand orange drinkable liquid is less than or equal to any like-sized sampling of Tang™ in nutritional value, and like Neon Genesis Evangelion it markets itself with shameless sex appeal. Get with it, guys! This is an anime fandom, not a geriatric community. God.

[]: Post by pwhodges » Mon Jan 20, 2014 6:14 am Oh, I don't know - some of us are pretty geriatric. But anyway, I think Tang is still sold, but OTOH I've never really been aware of it, because I think it was never sold here in the UK (if it was I never noticed it, for sure). So for me, Tang is just the informal word used around here for LCL, and it seems to serve that purpose perfectly well.

[]: Re: What's with all this "Tang" talk Post by Gorbatschow » Mon Jan 20, 2014 6:39 am I'll assume your serious although I'm really not sure. 1. Why should we care ? 2. NGE itself is over 15 years old. Just Also: inb4lock

[]: Post by Reichu » Mon Jan 20, 2014 8:19 am Trying to get people to stop calling LCL "Tang" would be like trying to get people to stop calling the new movies "Rebuild". Ain't gonna happen.

[]: Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Jan 20, 2014 10:52 am http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fsWvvmbDpTA

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jan 20, 2014 11:25 am This image is well over a decade old already.

[]: Post by Shinoyami65 » Mon Jan 20, 2014 11:37 am To be honest, 'tanged' is a lot shorter, simpler and more catchy than 'turned to orange goop by an omniscient blue-haired chick' or 'AT Field collapse'.

[]: Post by weeminator24 » Mon Jan 20, 2014 1:10 pm Where I work still sells Tang....so I'd say it's still relevant.

[]: Post by gatotsu911 » Mon Jan 20, 2014 4:31 pm Shinoyami65 wrote: To be honest, 'tanged' is a lot shorter, simpler and more catchy than 'turned to orange goop by an omniscient blue-haired chick' or 'AT Field collapse'.

[]: Post by Lavinius » Mon Jan 20, 2014 5:18 pm You know even though I already (loosely) knew what Tang was, when I first read about Eva on Tvtropes I assumed Tang was some sort of east Asian religious concept that I was supposed to know about. I supposed that it was a very common element in anime (like, say, Shinigami) but of course individual depictions varied and Eva's version of Tang was notable only in that it was orange.

[]: Post by gatotsu911 » Mon Jan 20, 2014 5:30 pm Lavinius wrote: when I first read about Eva on Tvtropes

[]: Post by Reichu » Mon Jan 20, 2014 5:46 pm Has this been left open long enough? ......Probably.

I mean, I remember Tang™ commercials just as well as the next kid who had to sit through them in between episodes of Pokemon , but god damn , do kids these days even know what Tang™ IS ?? Do they even sell that shit anymore?? Didn't they come to the attention of the FDA? I suggest that we update our terminology
